questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental right guaranteed by the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: It is guaranteed under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee that suggested the implementation of the three-language formula in India?
questions.explanation: The three-language formula was implemented in West Bengal as per the recommendations of the Kothari Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Indian Constitution introduced the concept of anti-defection law?
questions.explanation: The anti-defection law was introduced by the 52nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1985.

questions.question: In West Bengal, the Police Commmissioner is headed by?
questions.explanation: The CP (Commissioner of Police) in West Bengal is headed by the Police Commmissioner.

questions.question: Which of the following is a provision of the Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution provides for the creation of district councils.

questions.question: Who led the historic Chittagong Armory Raid in 1930?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was the mastermind behind the raid.

questions.question: Which famous Indian revolutionary was born in Barisal, Bengal in 1897?
questions.explanation: Sachindranath Sanyal was a key figure in the Indian independence movement.

questions.question: What was the name of the newspaper started by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay in Bengali in 1873?
questions.explanation: Tattwabodhini Patrika was a prominent Bengali newspaper.

questions.question: Who wrote the famous novel 'Durgeshnandini' in 1865?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay was a prominent Bengali novelist.

questions.question: Which of the following events marked the beginning of the Swadeshi movement in Bengal in 1905?
questions.explanation: The partition of Bengal triggered the Swadeshi movement.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region?
questions.explanation: It originates from the Chomutanne Himal in the Singalila Ridge.

questions.question: Which one of the following passes is located in the Sikkim Himalayas?
questions.explanation: It connects Sikkim to Tibet.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest mangrove forest in the world located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is the largest mangrove forest in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following districts is the largest district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It covers an area of 3,850 sq km.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain peaks is located in the Eastern part of the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region?
questions.explanation: It is the highest peak in India.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is launched by the Government of West Bengal for the welfare of SC/ST students?
options: Swami Vivekananda Merit Cum Means Scholarship
questions.explanation: It provides financial assistance to girls from SC/ST families.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the 'Mukhya Mantri Shramik Bima Yojana' launched by the Government of West Bengal?
options: To provide insurance coverage to unorganized sector workers
To provide financial assistance to small business owners
To provide loan at low interest rate to entrepreneurs
To provide employment opportunities to rural youth
questions.explanation: It provides insurance coverage to unorganized sector workers.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key component of the 'Sabuj Sangha' initiative launched by the Government of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It aims to promote sustainable livelihoods through natural resource management.

questions.question: The 'Karmayogi Prakalpa' is a scheme launched by the Government of West Bengal for the empowerment of?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide vocational training and employment opportunities to women.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary focus area of the 'Jai Bangla Health Mission' launched by the Government of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It aims to improve the health outcomes of women and children.

questions.question: By selling a scooter at a loss of 10%, a shopkeeper gets Rs. 4200. If he sells it at the marked price, he would get Rs. 4600. What is the cost price of the scooter?
questions.explanation: Selling price at loss is 90% of the cost price.

questions.question: In a shop in Kolkata, a TV set is marked at Rs. 18000. A customer buys it at 18% discount. How much amount will he have to pay?
questions.explanation: Discounted price is 82% of the marked price.

questions.question: Aman buys a mobile for Rs. 12000 and sells it for Rs. 11000. What is his loss percentage?
questions.explanation: Loss percentage is (loss/cost price)*100.

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchases a saree for Rs. 800 and sells it for Rs. 960. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is (profit/cost price)*100.

questions.question: A person gets a 12% commission on the sale of a product. If he sells a product for Rs. 15000, how much commission does he get?
questions.explanation: Commission is 12% of the selling price.

questions.question: A certain sum of money is invested in West Bengal Government Saving Bond at a compound interest rate of 8% per annum for 2 years. If the maturity amount is Rs 1,21,638, then the principal amount is?
questions.explanation: Compound interest formula

questions.question: A person bought a property in Kolkata for Rs 25 lakhs and sold it after 5 years at a simple interest rate of 6% per annum. The selling price of the property is Rs 40 lakhs. What percentage of profit has the person earned?
questions.explanation: Simple interest formula

questions.question: A company in West Bengal invests Rs 10 lakhs in a business for 3 years at a compound interest rate of 10% per annum. What is the amount of money that the company will have at the end of 3 years?
questions.explanation: Compound interest formula

questions.question: A certain sum of money is invested in a fixed deposit account in a bank in West Bengal for 2 years at a simple interest rate of 9% per annum. If the interest earned is Rs 17,880, then the principal amount is?
questions.explanation: Simple interest formula

questions.question: A West Bengal Government employee invested Rs 50,000 in a post office scheme at a compound interest rate of 12% per annum for 2 years. Find the amount of money the employee will get at the end of 2 years?
questions.explanation: Compound interest formula

questions.question: A worker can finish 1/4th of a work in 2 hours. How long will it take for 3 workers to complete the work?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of inverse proportion.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/3rd of a tank in 5 minutes. How long will it take to fill the remaining 2/3rd part of the tank?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of inverse proportion.

questions.question: 6 men can finish a work in 9 days. In how many days will 12 men finish the same work?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of inverse proportion.

questions.question: A tap can fill 1/5th of a bucket in 10 minutes. How long will it take to fill 3/5th of the bucket?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of inverse proportion.

questions.question: A person can finish 1/2nd of a work in 12 hours. How long will it take for 2 persons to complete the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Using the concept of inverse proportion.

questions.question: A train covers a distance of 120 km from Sealdah to Bandel in 2 hours. How much time will it take to cover a distance of 240 km?
questions.explanation: Distance is directly proportional to time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Kolkata to Durgapur covering a distance of 175 km in 5.5 hours. What is the speed of the bus in km/h?
questions.explanation: Speed is distance divided by time.

questions.question: A person travels from Dakshineswar to Howrah at an average speed of 20 km/h. If he reaches Howrah in 3 hours, what distance has he covered?
questions.explanation: Speed multiplied by time is equal to distance.

questions.question: A car travels from Siliguri to Jalpaiguri at an average speed of 25 km/h and covers a distance of 125 km in 5 hours. What is the ratio of time taken to distance travelled?
questions.explanation: Time is directly proportional to distance.

questions.question: A train leaves Howrah at 8:00 AM and reaches Asansol at 11:00 AM. If the distance between the two stations is 165 km and the train travels at a constant speed, what is the speed of the train in km/h?
questions.explanation: Speed is distance divided by time.

questions.question: A person is standing at a distance from his friend. To reach his friend he takes 7 steps forward and 3 steps backward. Then he takes 5 steps forward and 4 steps backward. How many steps will he take to reach his friend?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is found by calculating the net displacement.

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys fruits from a local market in Kolkata. He buys 240 kg of fruits at the rate of Rs. 40 per kg. What is the total cost of the fruits he bought?
questions.explanation: The total cost can be calculated by multiplying the weight of fruits with the cost per kg.

questions.question: If the word 'KOLKATA' is coded as 'K T A L O K', then the code for the word 'BENGAL' is?
questions.explanation: The code follows a pattern where each alphabetic character shifts one position forward in the alphabet.

questions.question: In a row of 8 houses, each house is painted with one of 8 different colors. The colors are - Red, Blue, Green, White, Yellow, Black, Grey, and Orange. What is the total number of ways to paint these 8 houses with different colors?
questions.explanation: The total number of ways can be calculated by using the concept of permutations.

questions.question: A man is standing in a queue. He moves 5 steps forward and 2 steps backward. Then he moves 3 steps forward and 4 steps backward. What is the net number of steps he moved?
questions.explanation: The net displacement is calculated by adding the total forward steps and total backward steps.

questions.question: Rajiv's wife is the sister of Kavita's husband, how is Kavita related to Rajiv?
questions.explanation: Kavita is Rajiv's sister-in-law.

questions.question: Dipti is the daughter of Kishore's wife's mother. How is Dipti related to Kishore?
questions.explanation: Dipti is Kishore's daughter.

questions.question: Pritam is the son of Kishore's father's brother's son. How is Pritam related to Kishore?
questions.explanation: Pritam is Kishore's cousin.

questions.question: Bidyut's wife's sister is married to Rakesh. How is Bikash related to Rakesh?
questions.explanation: Bikash is Rakesh's brother-in-law.

questions.question: Supriya is the daughter of Biman's wife's mother. How is Supriya related to Biman?
questions.explanation: Supriya is Biman's daughter.

questions.question: From the top of Howrah Bridge, if you walk 250 meters towards the north and then 150 meters towards the east, which direction is South-East?
questions.explanation: North and East directions were walked.

questions.question: If a person travels 8 km eastwards, then 2 km towards north and then 4 km towards west, how far and in which direction is the person now from the starting point?
questions.explanation: East and West directions cancel each other.

questions.question: A man is facing east direction. If he turns 135 degrees to his left, in which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He turned 135 degrees to his left.

questions.question: After walking 200 meters south and then 300 meters east, a person finds himself at a point 400 meters away from his starting point. In which direction is the starting point from his current position?
questions.explanation: Pythagorean Theorem can be used.

questions.question: A person is standing at a point X. He walks 12 km in the east direction and then 5 km in the north direction. After that, he walks 8 km in the west direction. In which direction is point X from his current position?
questions.explanation: East and West directions cancel each other.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the series: 3, 5, 7, 9, ?, 13
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is adding 2 to each number.

questions.question: Which of the following series would continue with 27, if the pattern is maintained?
questions.explanation: The series increases by 1 each time.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the series: 1, 2, 6, 24, ?, 144
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is squaring the number and then multiplying by 1, 2, 4, 8, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following series would continue with 27, if the pattern is maintained?
questions.explanation: The series increases by 1 each time.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the series: G, H, J, L, ?, P
questions.explanation: The series is based on the alphabetical order.
